title: GameNative adds multi-frame generation to PC games on Android devices, unlocking up to 100 fps gameplay Vulkan version of Lossless Scaling boosts performance
summary: GameNative's update brings Lossless Scaling's multi-frame generation to Android devices, enabling up to 100 FPS gameplay.
insight: Smoothened gaming experience with significantly reduced screen tearing.

title: College student hacks Taiwan high-speed rail line with software defined radios, stopping four trains 19 years without crypto key rotation ends in predictable result as hacker sails through 7 layers of protection
summary: A college student hacked the Taiwan high-speed rail line using software defined radios, stopping four trains. The breach occurred because of a predictable security vulnerability, specifically 19 years without crypto key rotation. This allowed the hacker to easily bypass multiple layers of protection.
insight: Outdated security practices create predictable vulnerabilities that hackers can exploit easily.

title: Samsung chip workers reject $340,000 one-time bonus, demand annual payouts like SK hynix's $900,000 workers want share of AI windfall, impending 18-day strike could cost Samsung up to $11.7 billion
summary: Samsung chip workers reject one-time bonus, demand annual payouts instead. This follows demands from SK hynix workers for a share of AI windfall. A potential 18-day strike could cost Samsung up to $11.7 billion.
insight: Workers seek long-term financial gains over one-time bonuses.

title: Save nearly $300 on AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D and Asus Prime RTX 5070 bundle, plus an 850W PSU score the core components to a new gaming rig for only $940
summary: Assemble a gaming PC with AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D and Asus Prime RTX 5070, along with an 850W PSU, for $940. This deal includes a copy of Capcom's Pragmata game. Build a high-performance gaming rig at a budget-friendly price.
insight: Affordable high-performance gaming PC build option for budget-conscious gamers.

title: 3D-printed rocket fuel successfully tested, could enable lighter missiles and faster production rates new additive manufacturing process tested at 1,800 PSI
summary: Chromatic 3D Materials successfully tested 3D-printed rocket propellant with 1,800 PSI pressure, enabling faster production and advanced designs.
insight: Potential for lightweight, high-performance missiles with rapid production rates.

title: PCIe 8.0 spec hits 1 TB/s of bandwidth and has new connector technology spec hits 0.5V milestone, final ratification expected in 2028
summary: PCIe 8.0 spec achieves 1 TB/s of bandwidth, with new connector technology reaching 0.5V milestone. The draft maintains 256 GT/s data transfer rate and backwards compatibility. Final ratification is expected in 2028.
insight: Revolutionizing data transfer speeds with unprecedented PCIe performance

title: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma kills Copilot for Gaming overhauls leadership with CoreAI veterans
summary: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has made significant changes to the gaming platform, killing off Copilot and introducing new leadership from CoreAI veterans. This shift aims to overhaul the gaming experience with AI-driven innovations. Sharma's vision is to drive growth and improvement.
insight: Xbox leadership pivots to AI-driven gaming with Copilot's demise

title: Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood
summary: Almost half of Americans oppose AI data center construction near their neighborhoods.
insight: Public concerns about AI data centers' environmental impact matter significantly.

title: Steam Controller and Puck CAD files officially released under a Creative Commons license Valve encourages users to create accessories for the device
summary: Valve has released official 3D CAD files for the Steam Controller and Puck, allowing users to create custom accessories. The files are available under a Creative Commons license, encouraging users to share their creations. This move enables enthusiasts to tailor their gaming experience.
insight: Custom accessories empower gamers to personalize their Steam experience.

title: New Linux StarFighter laptop family debuts starting at $1,878 Star Labs Systems' laptops arrive with spacious RAM, several options
summary: The StarFighter Linux laptop is now available for purchase, offering spacious RAM options. It's the latest model from Star Labs Systems, a small startup based in rural England. Prices start at $1,878.
insight: High-end Linux laptop offers competitive RAM capacity at an affordable price.

title: AMD to broaden and specialize EPYC CPUs, already working on Zen 7 architecture increased customization to better address evolving AI and cloud needs
summary: AMD plans to expand its EPYC CPU portfolio for data centers, targeting various workloads with increased customization and the upcoming Zen 7 architecture, to address evolving AI and cloud needs.
insight: Adaptable EPYC CPUs to dominate cloud and AI infrastructure markets.

title: SteelSeries Aerox 3 Wireless Gen 2 Review: The Bright and Bold
summary: The SteelSeries Aerox 3 Wireless Gen 2 offers an excellent gaming experience with fast charging and a bright, bold design. However, it may experience some sluggish performance. Overall, this mouse is a great choice for those seeking high-quality wireless gaming.
insight: Wireless gaming capabilities meet high-performance expectations with this mouse design

title: Dell XPS 16 (2026) Review: A flagship return to form
summary: Dell's revived XPS 16 offers an elite experience, but at a premium price.
insight: Flagship laptops require high investment for high-end performance.

title: Score this 500Hz 27-inch Samsung Odyssey OLED gaming monitor at its lowest ever price, now just $649.99 huge $349 saving nets you ultra-fast G60SF display with Nvidia G-Sync support
summary: The Samsung Odyssey OLED G60SF gaming monitor is now at $649.99, a $349 saving off the regular price, offering an ultra-fast G60SF display with Nvidia G-Sync support.
insight: Significant price drop for high-quality gaming monitor technology.

title: SpaceX files for $55 billion semiconductor fab in rural Texas for Musk's Terafab total chipmaking fab investment could reach $119 billion
summary: SpaceX files for a $55 billion semiconductor fab in rural Texas, exceeding the initial $20 billion Terafab investment announced in March. This filing suggests Musk's total chipmaking investment could reach $119 billion. The project's massive scale reflects growing demand for advanced semiconductors.
insight: Transformative investment in chipmaking could drive technological breakthroughs and innovation

title: Apple quietly axes 128GB Mac Studio amid supply constraints and local AI frenzy highest memory capacity reduced to 96GB, two months after discontinuation of 512GB model
summary: Apple discreetly removes high capacity Mac Studios and Mac minis from sale due to ongoing memory constraints.
insight: Memory supply constraints impact consumer Apple products severely.

title: FalCAN Probe is an open-source, STM32-based USB to CAN/RS-485/RS-422 adapter
summary: FalCAN Probe is an open-source, multi-protocol USB adapter for CAN and RS serial buses, featuring an STM32F042 microcontroller with USB, CAN, RS-485, and RS-422 interfaces.
insight: Flexible, open-source adapter enables diverse automotive and industrial network connectivity options.

title: Ploopy Bean open-source hardware pointing stick mouse runs QMK firmware
summary: The Ploopy Bean is a 3D-printed, open-source pointing stick mouse that runs QMK firmware on a Raspberry Pi RP2040 microcontroller, featuring high-precision 3D hall effect sensor and customizable buttons.
insight: Advances open-source hardware innovation with precise and customizable pointing technology.

title: HAUI 3Gang Touch Display is a 7-inch wall-mount Home Assistant dashboard with MQTT support
summary: The HAUI 3Gang Touch Display is a 7-inch wall-mount smart home control dashboard designed to run Home Assistant or other web-based home automation dashboards. It features a Raspberry Pi 3B+, 7-inch capacitive touchscreen, Wi-Fi connectivity, and built-in MQTT integration. The device replaces standard wall switches and fits into a standard single-gang electrical box.
insight: Enhances home automation control with a customizable, wall-mountable dashboard solution.

title: 2026 Best of Sensors winners announced at Sensors Converge
summary: The Best of Sensors awards at Sensors Converge celebrated innovation in sensor technology, recognizing industry leaders and pioneers. The winners showcased cutting-edge advancements in various fields, from wearable technology to automotive systems. These achievements highlight the transformative impact of sensors on modern industries.
insight: Sensors Converge recognizes game-changing innovations in sensor technology and applications.

title: Sensors Converge 26: smart glasses, robots, Edge AI predictions
summary: Thousands attended the first day of SC exhibition floor in Santa Clara, experiencing smart glasses, robots, and Edge AI predictions.
insight: Convergence of sensors, AI, and robotics transforms industries and lives.

title: Student brought multiple Taiwan high-speed trains to a standstill with handheld radio spoofing attack
summary: A 48-minute disruption occurred on Taiwan's high-speed rail due to a hoax alert. A student's handheld radio spoofing attack triggered a General Alarm signal, prompting emergency stops. The incident affected four trains, with some reports saying three.
insight: Cybersecurity threats can cripple critical infrastructure with devastating consequences quickly.

title: Microsoft made Copilot a co-author on every VS Code project, reverted after developers revolted
summary: Microsoft inadvertently made Copilot a co-author in VS Code projects, sparking user backlash.
insight: Developer ownership and authorship are being redefined by AI tools automatically.

title: Hackers used Daemon Tools' own website to silently install backdoors on thousands of PCs for nearly a month
summary: Hackers compromised Daemon Tools' website, silently installing backdoors on thousands of PCs.
insight: Legitimate digital certificates used to bypass traditional security detection methods.

title: Myst and Riven remakes head to PlayStation, Xbox, and Microsoft Store
summary: Myst and Riven remakes are coming to PS5, Xbox, and Microsoft Store. The games will support HDR, 4K, and ray tracing for immersive experiences. This remake marks a return to classic puzzle-adventure gameplay.
insight: Reviving nostalgic puzzle-adventure games for modern gaming platforms

title: Roku and TCL face lawsuit over software updates that allegedly brick smart TVs
summary: Roku and TCL face a lawsuit over software updates that allegedly render certain smart TVs unusable, affecting Roku Select and Plus Series sets, as well as TCL's 3- to 6-series TVs running Roku OS.
insight: Uncontrolled software updates can cause irreversible damage to smart devices.

title: Game engines were built for video games, but Japan is finding more interesting things to do with them
summary: Japanese developers are repurposing popular 3D engines for new applications beyond gaming, expanding their capabilities. Unity and Unreal Engine are being used for non-game projects, such as simulations and training tools. These engines are being given a 'extended life'.
insight: Repurposing game engines unlocks new possibilities in innovation and development.

title: The quiet reinvention of search in the age of AI
summary: The concept of traditional search is evolving with AI advancements, leading to a quieter, more nuanced approach to information retrieval. New technologies, such as generative AI, are transforming the search experience. This shift requires a reevaluation of how we interact with information.
insight: AI redefines search from finding answers to generating new possibilities

title: A single Micron SSD can now store 245TB and use half the power of hard drives
summary: Micron's 6600 ION SSD offers up to 245TB storage in a single drive, doubling industry capacity. The new SSD comes in U.2 and E3.L form factors. It also uses half the power of traditional hard drives.
insight: Revolutionary storage density boosts capacity and efficiency for data-intensive applications.

title: Apple is paying $250 million after overselling AI features before they actually worked
summary: Apple is paying $250 million to settle claims it oversold AI features. Consumers who bought certain iPhone models between June 2024 and March 2025 may be eligible for up to $95 per device. Apple denies any wrongdoing as part of the settlement.
insight: Marketing hype can lead to costly product feature disappointments

title: Scientists create "living plastic" that can self-destruct on command
summary: Scientists have created a 'living plastic' with a built-in self-destruct mechanism. The material contains dormant spores from engineered Bacillus subtilis bacteria. This allows for the material to degrade on command.
insight: Breakaway from non-biodegradable plastics with biodegradable alternatives now possible.

title: Microsoft Edge stores all your saved passwords unencrypted in memory
summary: Microsoft Edge stores saved passwords unencrypted in memory, compromising user security.
insight: Unencrypted passwords in memory are vulnerable to memory scraping attacks.

title: AMD warns gaming revenue will plunge over 20% as memory prices drive up PC hardware costs
summary: AMD's Q1 revenue rose 38% to $10.3 billion, driven by strong data center sales and improved client and gaming segments. Gaming revenue saw a notable increase. However, the company warns that gaming revenue will decline due to rising memory costs.
insight: Rising memory prices threaten AMD's gaming revenue growth momentum.

title: A major study claiming ChatGPT improves student learning has been retracted
summary: A major study claiming ChatGPT improves student learning has been retracted. The paper, published in May 2025, combined results from 51 separate studies. It had already gained significant attention before being retracted.
insight: Questionable research methods undermine AI education credibility

title: Microsoft kills Gaming Copilot for Xbox consoles and mobile
summary: Microsoft has canceled the rollout of Gaming Copilot for Xbox consoles and mobile devices, just two months after announcing its release for current-gen consoles.
insight: Gaming industry faces uncertainty with cancelled AI-powered features.
